segments.beat: Hand holds device face-on; menu screen visible with cyan text, transparent shell and PCB internals clearly visible
Slight reposition; display shows menu items, D-pad and see-through body prominent in frame
Display goes dim/blank transition; transparent shell with orange flex-cable PCB fully exposed
Game pixels appear — Pong/paddle-style game running on transparent display; retro gameplay active
Game continues; 'Timer' screen with cyan score/stats text; hand holds device two-handed showing pocket scale
Device rotated to show rear/side angle; PCB substrate, button hardware, and shell construction visible
Front-face angle re-established; game running, transparent pane shows lit pixels against see-through body
Slow pan/tilt; clear resin shell texture and button layout (D-pad + A/B) shown in detail
Continued gameplay demo; device fills frame, copper flex trace and chip silhouettes visible through shell
Final frames: menu/score screen returns; device set down or held flat; cyan text on transparent display closes clip

demonstrates.motion_value: high — continuous handling keeps PCB visible through shell across the clip
high — live game pixels lit on the transparent pane
medium — Pong/paddle gameplay actively running on screen
medium — resin shell and button hardware visible on rotation
low — flex cable and chip visible through shell

selection_note: Strong continuous transparency + live gameplay loop in under 30s; PCB internals visible throughout and game pixels on transparent pane land both load-bearing claims simultaneously.

aesthetics.standout: cyan display glow pops against neutral grey
aesthetics.weakness: prototype PCB guts and handheld wobble dominate every frame
aesthetics.note: 27s handheld close-up of bare dev board; no styled environment, no tripod, no hero moment
